methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Axibo AI is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised C$12M to date. The valuation universe consists of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ), including established names such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. The robotics and manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, though direct comparables at identical stage and valuation tier are limited.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable for this analysis. The dataset identified zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-tier valuation peers meeting the three-datapoint minimum threshold. Reliance on all-sector medians would risk conflating seed-stage capital structures with more mature venture or late-stage entities, introducing material distortion. Consequently, no triangulated valuation range can be supported by the available comparable data.

The analysis reflects a fundamental constraint in frontier-tech robotics valuations at early stage: sparse, heterogeneous peer sets limit quantitative cross-sectional methods. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow peer anchoring, the temporal and operational variance across the top private peers cited, and the relevance of the single public comparable to seed-stage risk profiles. Further data collection on recent seed and Series A transactions in robotics, or qualitative assessment of Axibo's technological differentiation and capital efficiency, would be necessary to establish a defensible valuation range.
